commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair lane is needed because there is no discrete code defect; the remaining action is maintainer review, CI, and key-contract acceptance for this open PR.; Cleared: The diff changes TypeScript routing logic, docs/help text, and tests only; it does not alter workflows, dependencies, secrets handling, package execution, or downloaded artifacts. (sha=93d3a890ea16c1001c471c67ea620e120cf8a891)
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands the existing `channels.modelByChannel` resolver so direct-message peer identifiers can select provider/model overrides, wires the reply/status/agent-command callers, and updates docs/help text plus resolver tests.

PR surface: Source +53, Tests +244, Docs +15. Total +312 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main has no direct peer parameter in the model override resolver, while the PR adds direct peer candidates and documents DM key forms.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed because there is no discrete code defect; the remaining action is maintainer review, CI, and key-contract acceptance for this open PR.

target.title: feat(channels): add directUserId support for per-DM model override
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`channels.modelByChannel` could select provider/model overrides for group conversations (`-100123`) and wildcard (`*`), but not for individual DM peers. Operators had no way to pin different models per user in direct messages.

## Changes

### Core: `src/channels/model-overrides.ts`
- **`directUserId` → `directUserIds` array**: Callers now pass all available identity fields as an array; the resolver tries each one until a key match is found.
- **`expandPeerIds`**: When a candidate has a `<channel>:` prefix (e.g. `telegram:12345`), the resolver also tries the unprefixed form (`12345`), so stored-session paths that only persist channel-prefixed IDs can match raw config keys.
- **Direct-chat gate**: `resolveDirectChannelModelMatch` only runs when `normalizeChatType(params.groupChatType) === "direct"`, preventing DM key leakage into group/channel messages.

### Callers
| File | What changed |
|------|-------------|
| `get-reply.ts` | Live reply path — passes all 6 identity fields from stored session + finalized context |
| `dispatch-from-config.ts` | Same as get-reply |
| `status-message.ts` | Stored-session path — passes origin fields (prefixes expanded by `expandPeerIds`) |
| `agent-command.ts` | Stored-session path — same approach |

### Docs
- `docs/gateway/config-channels.md`: DM key-forms table for all major channels
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`: Help text updated to "channel id / DM peer id"
- `src/config/types.channels.ts`: JSDoc updated

## Evidence

### Live Feishu DM test

Gateway config using `modelByChannel` with Feishu DM override:
```
feishu:
  "feishu:ou_a8b6cab7e945387de5f253775d9b4d85": tencent/kimi-k2.5
  "*": tencent/glm-5
```

Gateway log confirms DM peer override selected:
```
model=kimi-k2.5  ← from feishu DM specific entry, not wildcard glm-5
```

Config channel: `feishu`
User ID format: `feishu:ou_...` (with channel prefix)
Default wildcard model: `glm-5`

See PR comment with before/after gateway log output.

## Tests

### `model-overrides.test.ts` — 19 tests

| Category | Tests | What it covers |
|----------|-------|---------------|
| Group/parent matching | 4 | groupId, topic suffix, parent session key, channel name fallback |
| Plugin conversation | 2 | plugin-owned conversation resolution |
| Wildcard direct | 1 | `*` works for direct chats |
| DM matching (single) | 5 | directUserId match, wildcard fallback, origin.from match, group precedence, non-direct gating |
| DM matching (multi-candidate) | 4 | Slack/Discord/Telegram channel-specific forms, candidate priority |
| Prefix expansion | 3 | telegram/discord prefix stripping, cross-channel non-match |

### Agent-command live-model-switch — 65 tests (all passing)
### Slack extension prepare — 85 tests (all passing)
target.author: xydigit-zt
